pages.about.aboutMeText1: I'm Sebastian, a product and technology generalist based in Germany. I've spent my career at the intersection of strategy, product development, and applied technology — working with early-stage projects and established teams alike to build things that actually matter.
pages.about.aboutMeText2: My interest in knowledge work, thinking tools, and AI led me to Obsidian — and eventually to building Obsilo Agent: an autonomous AI operating layer for your vault that respects your privacy, runs on local or remote models of your choice, and stays fully open source.
pages.about.getInTouchText: I built Obsilo for myself, for my needs and requirements. I am open to feedback and grateful for suggestions, but I can't promise anything. The best way to reach me is via LinkedIn or by opening a discussion on GitHub. For commercial enquiries or collaborations: sebastian@obsilo.ai.
pages.about.supportText: Obsilo is and will remain open source and free to use. I build and maintain it independently in my spare time. If it saves you time or adds value to your workflow, consider supporting its continued development with a small monthly contribution. Completely optional, always appreciated ❤️.
pages.about.title: Sebastian Hanke
pages.about.whyObsiloText: I wanted an AI that works inside my knowledge system rather than alongside it. One that understands my notes, follows my workflows, and can actually do things — not just answer questions. Working with Kilo Code in VS Code inspired me what agentic loops should look like when operating inside another tool. Obsilo Agent is that idea applied to knowledge work.
pages.checkpoints.configuration.table: SettingDefaultDescription Enable CheckpointsOnMaster toggle. When off, no snapshots are taken and restore is unavailable. Checkpoint Timeout30 secondsMaximum time to wait for a snapshot to complete. If exceeded, the task continues without a checkpoint. Auto-cleanupOnAutomatically remove old checkpoints to save disk space.
pages.checkpoints.diskUsage.text: The shadow repo uses standard git compression. For most vaults, each checkpoint adds a few kilobytes. With auto-cleanup enabled, old checkpoints are pruned automatically. You can also manually clear checkpoints from Settings → Backup.
pages.checkpoints.howItWorks.text: Obsilo maintains a shadow git repository at:
pages.checkpoints.howItWorks.text2: This is separate from your vault's own git history (if you use git for your vault). It uses isomorphic-git — a pure JavaScript git implementation that requires no native git binary.
pages.checkpoints.lifecycle.list:
• Task starts — you send a message and the agent begins working
  • First write detected — before executing the first write_file, edit_file, delete_file, or similar operation, Obsilo takes a snapshot of all currently-tracked files
  • Task completes — the checkpoint is retained and listed in Settings → Backup
  • Auto-cleanup (if enabled) — old checkpoints beyond the retention limit are removed
pages.checkpoints.restoring.callout: Restore only affects changed files Restoring a checkpoint reverts only the files that were modified in that specific task — not the entire vault. Files created by the task are moved to trash; files edited are restored to their pre-edit content.
pages.checkpoints.restoring.list:
• Go to Settings → Advanced → Backup
  • You'll see a list of checkpoints, ordered by date, with the task that created them
  • Click Preview on a checkpoint to see a diff of what changed
  • Click Restore to revert the files changed in that task back to their pre-task state
pages.checkpoints.subtitle: Checkpoints give you a safety net for every agent task. Before the first write operation in a task, Obsilo automatically takes a snapshot of all tracked files. If something goes wrong, you can restore to that snapshot with one click.
pages.checkpoints.title: Checkpoints
pages.checkpoints.vsVaultGit.list:
• Non-invasive: Checkpoints don't pollute your vault's git history with agent commits
  • No git required: Works even if you don't use git for your vault at all
  • Automatic: Happens without any user action — no need to remember to commit before each agent task
  • Scoped: Restores only the files a specific task touched, not the whole vault
  • Task-linked: Each checkpoint is linked to the conversation that created it, making it easy to understand what changed and why
pages.checkpoints.vsVaultGit.text: If you already use git for your vault, you might wonder why Obsilo has its own checkpoint system. The reasons:
pages.checkpoints.whatIsTracked.list:
• Any file the agent has touched can be restored
  • Files in .obsidian-agentignore are not tracked
  • The checkpoint doesn't restore files to a vault-wide snapshot — it restores only the files changed in that task
pages.checkpoints.whatIsTracked.text: The shadow repo tracks all files that the agent has read or written in previous tasks. On the first snapshot, it also includes all files in the vault root (excluding ignored paths). This means:

pages.mcpServers.addingServer.text: Go to Settings → Agent Behaviour → MCP and click Add Server. Fill in the server configuration:
pages.mcpServers.alwaysAllow.text: The alwaysAllow field on a server config lists tool names that never require approval. For example, if you always trust reads from a filesystem server:
pages.mcpServers.alwaysAllow.text2: All other tools from that server still follow normal approval rules.
pages.mcpServers.configurationFields.tab: FieldTypeDescription typestringTransport: "stdio", "sse", or "streamable-http" commandstring(stdio only) The command to run (e.g., "node", "npx", "python") argsstring[](stdio only) Command arguments envobject(stdio only) Environment variables injected into the process urlstring(sse/http) Server URL headersobject(sse/http) HTTP headers (e.g., Authorization) timeoutnumberConnection timeout in ms (default: 30000) alwaysAllowstring[]Tool names on this server that never require user approval disabledbooleanTemporarily disable this server without removing it
pages.mcpServers.fetch.text: Enhanced web fetching with cookie support:
pages.mcpServers.github.text: Access GitHub repositories, issues, and PRs:
pages.mcpServers.security.callout: MCP servers run with system permissions stdio MCP servers run as local processes with the same permissions as Obsidian. Only connect servers you trust. The MCP tool requires approval by default — enable auto-approval only for trusted servers.
pages.mcpServers.security.list:
• Review what each server's tools can do before enabling auto-approval
  • Use env to pass API keys rather than embedding them in command args
  • Use per-mode whitelisting to limit which modes can access powerful tools
pages.mcpServers.subtitle: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) lets you connect external tools and data sources to Obsilo Agent. MCP servers expose tools that the agent can call just like built-in tools.
pages.mcpServers.title: MCP Servers
pages.mcpServers.toolDiscovery.text: When Obsilo connects to an MCP server, it automatically queries the server's tool list. All discovered tools are then listed in the agent's system prompt so the agent knows they exist and how to use them. Tools appear as:
pages.mcpServers.transports.table: TransportHow it worksUse case stdioObsilo launches a local process and communicates via stdin/stdoutLocal tools, scripts, CLI programs SSEConnects to a remote server via Server-Sent Events (HTTP GET)Remote services, hosted tools streamable-httpBidirectional HTTP streamingModern remote MCP servers
pages.mcpServers.whatIsMcp.list:
• Calendar and task management (Google Calendar, Todoist)
  • Code execution and analysis
  • Database access
  • File system operations outside the vault
  • External API integrations (GitHub, Slack, Notion)
  • Custom automation scripts
pages.mcpServers.whatIsMcp.text: MCP is an open protocol for connecting AI models to external tools and resources. An MCP server is a small program that exposes a set of tools (with JSON schemas) and executes them when called. Obsilo connects to MCP servers and makes their tools available to the agent via the use_mcp_tool tool.
pages.mcpServers.whatIsMcp.text2: Examples of what MCP servers can provide:
pages.mcpServers.whitelisting.list:
• Go to Settings → Modes → (edit a mode) → Allowed MCP Servers
  • Select which servers this mode can use
  • If none are selected, all configured servers are allowed
pages.mcpServers.whitelisting.text: You can restrict which MCP servers are available in specific modes:
pages.mcpServers.whitelisting.text2: This lets you give a research mode access to a web-search MCP server while restricting other modes from it.
pages.settingsRef.backupTab.text: Browse and restore from task checkpoints. Each entry shows the task, timestamp, and files changed. Use Preview to see a diff and Restore to revert those files.
pages.settingsRef.chatHistorySettings.ta: SettingDefaultDescription Chat History Folder(empty)Vault folder to save conversations to. Empty = disabled. Creates JSON files per conversation.
pages.settingsRef.checkpointsSettings.ta: SettingDefaultDescription Enable CheckpointsOnTake automatic snapshots before task write operations. Checkpoint Timeout30sMax time to wait for a snapshot. If exceeded, task continues without checkpoint. Auto-cleanupOnRemove old checkpoints automatically.
pages.settingsRef.debugTab.table: SettingDefaultDescription Debug ModeOffEnable verbose console logging for troubleshooting. Output visible in Obsidian developer tools (Ctrl+Shift+I).
pages.settingsRef.embeddingsTab.table: SettingDescription Embedding ModelModel used to generate vectors for semantic search. Changing this requires rebuilding the index. Add Custom Embedding ModelAdd a Xenova/transformers model by name or an API-based embedding model.
pages.settingsRef.interfaceTab.table: SettingDefaultDescription Sidebar Positionright"left" or "right" — which side the Obsilo panel appears on. Press Enter to sendOnWhen on, Enter sends the message and Shift+Enter inserts a newline. When off, Ctrl/Cmd+Enter sends. Auto-add active file contextOnAutomatically include the currently open note as context in every message. Show welcome messageOnShow a welcome message when the sidebar first opens. Include current time in contextOnInject today's date and time into the system prompt.
pages.settingsRef.logsTab.text: View the operation audit trail. Logs are JSONL files at .obsidian/plugins/obsilo-agent/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl. Select a date to view that day's log. Retained for 30 days.
pages.settingsRef.loopTab.table: SettingDefaultDescription Consecutive Mistake Limit3Stop the agent after N consecutive tool errors. 0 = disabled. Rate Limit (ms)0Minimum milliseconds between API iterations. 0 = no limit. Use to avoid rate limit errors. Context CondensingOffAutomatically summarize conversation when estimated tokens exceed the threshold. Condensing Threshold80%% of the model's context window at which to trigger condensing (50–95). Power Steering Frequency0Inject a mode-role reminder every N iterations to keep the agent on task. 0 = disabled.
pages.settingsRef.mcpTab.table: SettingDescription MCP Servers listAll configured servers with name, type, and connection status. Add ServerAdd a new MCP server configuration. Active Servers whitelistRestrict which configured servers are globally active. Empty = all allowed. Connect / DisconnectManually connect or disconnect a server.
pages.settingsRef.modelsTab.table: SettingDescription Active Models listList of all configured models. Each entry shows provider, name, and enabled status. Add Custom ModelAdd a new model with any provider type. Set name, provider, API key, base URL, temperature, max tokens. Global Custom InstructionsText appended to the system prompt for ALL modes. Use for universal formatting rules, language preferences, etc. Default ModelThe model selected by default in new chats. Set by enabling and selecting a model.
pages.settingsRef.modesTab.table: SettingDescription Built-in modesAsk and Agent modes are always present. Their role definitions can be viewed but not edited. Custom Modes listUser-created modes (vault or global scope). New ModeCreate a custom mode with slug, name, icon, description, role definition, custom instructions, tool groups, and scope. Per-mode ModelOverride the default model for a specific mode. Per-mode ToolsRestrict available tools to a subset of the mode's tool groups. Per-mode MCP ServersWhitelist specific MCP servers for this mode. Forced SkillsSkills always injected for this mode regardless of keyword matching. Forced WorkflowWorkflow applied to every message in this mode (unless message starts with /).
pages.settingsRef.permissionsTab.table: SettingDefaultDescription Enable Auto-ApprovalOffMaster toggle. When off, every operation requires manual approval. Show quick-toggle bar in chatOnDisplay permission toggles at the top of the chat view. Read operationsAuto-approvedread_file, list_files, search_files, and vault intelligence tools. Note editsManualwrite_file, edit_file, append_to_file, update_frontmatter. Vault changesManualcreate_folder, delete_file, move_file. Web operationsManualweb_fetch, web_search. MCP toolsManualuse_mcp_tool. Mode switchingManualswitch_mode. SubtasksManualnew_task (spawn sub-agents). QuestionsAuto-approvedask_followup_question (shows question card directly without approval step). Todo updatesAuto-approvedupdate_todo_list.
pages.settingsRef.promptsTab.table: SettingDescription Custom Prompts listAll user-defined slash-command prompt templates. NameDisplay name for the prompt. SlugThe /slug used to invoke the prompt. ContentPrompt template text. Supports {{userInput}} and {{activeFile}} variables. EnabledToggle: shown in autocomplete and functional when enabled. Mode RestrictionOptional: only show this prompt in a specific mode.
pages.settingsRef.rulesTab.table: SettingDescription Rules listAll .md and .txt files in .obsidian-agent/rules/. Toggle each on/off. New RuleCreate a new rule file with an inline editor. Edit / DeleteManage existing rule files.
pages.settingsRef.semanticIndex.table: SettingDefaultDescription Enable Semantic IndexOffMaster toggle for semantic search. Must be on for semantic_search tool to work. Auto-IndexneverWhen to automatically rebuild: "startup", "mode-switch", or "never". Auto-Index on ChangeOffRe-index files as they change. Can cause UI lag with local models on large vaults. Build Index—Button: start a full (incremental) index build now. Chunk Size2000Target characters per chunk. Smaller = more precise. Changing requires full rebuild. Batch Size20Files per disk commit during indexing. Excluded Folders—Comma-separated folders to skip (e.g., "Templates, Archive"). Index PDFsOffExtract and index text from PDF files. HyDEOffGenerate hypothetical document before query. Costs 1 extra LLM call per search. Storage Locationobsidian-sync"obsidian-sync" = syncs with Obsidian Sync; "local" = device-only.
pages.settingsRef.skillsTab.table: SettingDescription Skills listAll discovered SKILL.md files. Shows name, description (used for keyword matching), and match count. New SkillCreate a new skill folder and SKILL.md file. Edit DescriptionUpdate the keyword-matching description without editing the skill file directly.
pages.settingsRef.subtitle: Complete reference for every setting in Obsilo Agent. Open via Obsidian Settings → Obsilo Agent.
pages.settingsRef.title: Settings Reference
pages.settingsRef.webSearchSettings.tabl: SettingDefaultDescription Enable Web ToolsOffEnable web_fetch and web_search tools. Search Providernone"brave", "tavily", or "none" (web_search disabled if none). Brave API Key—API key for Brave Search. Tavily API Key—API key for Tavily Search.
pages.settingsRef.workflowsTab.table: SettingDescription Workflows listAll .md and .txt files in .obsidian-agent/workflows/. Toggle each on/off. Shows the slash command slug. New WorkflowCreate a new workflow file with an inline editor. Edit / DeleteManage existing workflow files.
